journal_entry.reason: Cash borrowed from bank with corresponding long-term loan liability at face value

output.reasoning: Borrowing transaction (loan principal) is not a taxable supply; interest expense is also not subject to sales tax

output.reason: Cash borrowed from bank with corresponding long-term loan liability at face value

entry_drafter.reason: Cash borrowed from bank with corresponding long-term loan liability at face value

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 3-year term loan is non-current liability; the portion due within 12 months (interest payments) would be current, but the principal is long-term.
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Under IAS 1, liabilities are classified as non-current when the entity has an unconditional right to defer settlement for at least 12 months — a 3-year term loan is non-current.

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Cash received equals face value of $200,000 — no discount or premium mentioned, so loan is recorded at face value.
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Under IFRS 9, financial liabilities are initially measured at fair value; when cash received equals face value with market-rate interest, fair value equals face value.

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: This is the borrowing transaction — interest accrual entries are separate future entries as interest accrues.
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 9 requires effective interest method for subsequent measurement, but initial recognition is at transaction amount received.

best_attempt.reason: Initial borrowing entry records cash received and loan liability at face value; future interest entries are separate transactions.

output_tax_specialist.reasoning: Borrowing transaction (loan principal) is not a taxable supply; interest expense is also not subject to sales tax
